Title: Rheumatoid Arthritis RCT: Treatment vs Placebo by Age Group — Koch & Edwards 1988
Source: Koch, G. G. and Edwards, S. (1988). Clinical efficacy trials with categorical data. In K. E. Peace (ed.), Biopharmaceutical Statistics for Drug Development. R vcd::Arthritis.
note: Treatment is superior to Placebo in both Young (<55 years) and Old (>=55 years) patient groups. Young patients: 50.0% vs 13.0%; Old patients: 75.9% vs 55.0%. Age is associated with higher placebo response rates in older patients but does not reverse the treatment advantage. The pooled treatment advantage (68.3% vs 32.6%) is confirmed in both strata.

strata.Description: Patients 55 years of age or older
